title: chloraea grandiflora
intro: Chloraea grandiflora, also known as the "tree daisy" or "bell orchid", is a species of orchid native to New Zealand. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Chloraea grandiflora include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and can also be used in poultices or ointments to apply its medicinal properties directly to the skin.
